name: react
description: A declarative, efficient, and flexible JavaScript library for building user interfaces.
readmeSummary: React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces. It is maintained by Facebook and a community of individual developers and companies. React can be used as a base in the development of single-page or mobile applications.

issues.title: Bug: useState not updating correctly in strict mode
Performance: Memoization overhead in large lists

name: vue
description: The Progressive JavaScript Framework
readmeSummary: Vue is a progressive framework for building user interfaces. Unlike other monolithic frameworks, Vue is designed from the ground up to be incrementally adoptable. The core library is focused on the view layer only.

issues.title: v-model binding issue with nested objects

name: tensorflow
description: An Open Source Machine Learning Framework for Everyone
readmeSummary: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. It has a comprehensive, flexible ecosystem of tools, libraries, and community resources that lets researchers push the state-of-the-art in ML.

name: kubernetes
description: Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management
readmeSummary: Kubernetes is an open source container orchestration engine for automating deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications. The open source project is hosted by the Cloud Native Computing Foundation.

name: rust
description: Empowering everyone to build reliable and efficient software.
readmeSummary: Rust is a systems programming language that runs blazingly fast, prevents segfaults, and guarantees thread safety. This is the main source code repository for Rust. It contains the compiler, standard library, and documentation.

issues.title: Borrow checker false positive with NLL

name: spring-boot
description: Spring Boot helps you to create Spring-powered, production-grade applications
readmeSummary: Spring Boot makes it easy to create stand-alone, production-grade Spring based Applications that you can just run. We take an opinionated view of the Spring platform so that you can get started with minimum fuss.

name: next.js
description: The React Framework for Production
readmeSummary: Next.js is a React framework that gives you building blocks to create web applications. By framework, we mean Next.js handles the tooling and configuration needed for React, and provides additional structure, features, and optimizations.

issues.title: App Router: cache invalidation not working

name: vscode
description: Visual Studio Code
readmeSummary: Visual Studio Code is a lightweight but powerful source code editor which runs on your desktop and is available for Windows, macOS and Linux. It comes with built-in support for JavaScript, TypeScript and Node.js.

issues.title: TypeScript suggestions slow in large files

name: django
description: The Web framework for perfectionists with deadlines.
readmeSummary: Django is a high-level Python Web framework that encourages rapid development and clean, pragmatic design. Built by experienced developers, it takes care of much of the hassle of Web development.

name: gin
description: Gin is a HTTP web framework written in Go
readmeSummary: Gin is a web framework written in Go. It features a martini-like API with performance that is up to 40 times faster thanks to httprouter. If you need performance and good productivity, you will love Gin.

name: node
description: Node.js JavaScript runtime
readmeSummary: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine. Node.js uses an event-driven, non-blocking I/O model that makes it lightweight and efficient.

name: flutter
description: Flutter makes it easy and fast to build beautiful apps
readmeSummary: Flutter is Google's SDK for crafting beautiful, fast user experiences for mobile, web, and desktop from a single codebase. Flutter works with existing code, is used by developers and organizations around the world.

issues.title: Scroll jank on iOS with large lists

name: pytorch
description: Tensors and Dynamic neural networks in Python with strong GPU acceleration
readmeSummary: PyTorch is a Python package that provides two high-level features: Tensor computation with strong GPU acceleration and Deep neural networks built on a tape-based autograd system.

name: actix-web
description: A powerful, pragmatic, and extremely fast web framework for Rust
readmeSummary: Actix Web is a powerful, pragmatic, and extremely fast web framework for Rust. Typesafe and feature rich, it provides services, routing, middleware, and many more features out of the box.

name: nuxt
description: The Intuitive Vue Framework
readmeSummary: Nuxt is a free and open-source framework with an intuitive and extendable way to create type-safe, performant and production-grade full-stack web applications and websites with Vue.js.

name: react-native
description: A framework for building native applications using React
readmeSummary: React Native brings React's declarative UI paradigm to iOS and Android. With React Native, you use native components and modules, and build your app the same way you build a React web app.

name: webpack
description: A bundler for javascript and friends
readmeSummary: Webpack is a static module bundler for modern JavaScript applications. When webpack processes your application, it internally builds a dependency graph from one or more entry points.

issues.title: Tree shaking not working with re-exports
Source maps with eval mode broken

name: fastapi
description: FastAPI framework, high performance, easy to learn, fast to code, ready for production
readmeSummary: FastAPI is a modern, fast web framework for building APIs with Python based on standard Python type hints. Key features include automatic docs, dependency injection, WebSocket support, and more.

name: tokio
description: A runtime for writing reliable asynchronous applications with Rust
readmeSummary: Tokio is an event-driven, non-blocking I/O platform for writing asynchronous applications with the Rust programming language. At a high level, it provides a few major components for async I/O.

name: nest
description: A progressive Node.js framework for building efficient and scalable server-side applications.
readmeSummary: Nest is a framework for building efficient, scalable Node.js server-side applications. It uses progressive JavaScript, is built with and fully supports TypeScript and combines elements of OOP, FP, and FRP.

name: three.js
description: JavaScript 3D Library
readmeSummary: Three.js is a cross-browser JavaScript library and application programming interface used to create and display animated 3D computer graphics in a web browser using WebGL.

name: pandas
description: Flexible and powerful data analysis / manipulation library for Python
readmeSummary: pandas is a fast, powerful, flexible and easy to use open source data analysis and manipulation tool, built on top of the Python programming language.

name: dotnet
description: .NET is a cross-platform runtime for cloud, IoT, and desktop apps
readmeSummary: .NET is a free, cross-platform, open source developer platform for building many different types of applications. With .NET you can target any application type running on any platform.

name: svelte
description: Cybernetically enhanced web apps
readmeSummary: Svelte is a radical new approach to building user interfaces. Whereas traditional frameworks like React and Vue do the bulk of their work in the browser, Svelte shifts that work into a compile step.

name: rocket
description: A web framework for Rust that makes simple web apps fun
readmeSummary: Rocket is an async web framework for Rust with a focus on usability, security, extensibility, and speed. It provides first-class support for features like typed URIs, form handling, and state management.

name: express
description: Fast, unopinionated, minimalist web framework for node.
readmeSummary: Fast, unopinionated, minimalist web framework for Node.js. Express is a minimal and flexible Node.js web application framework that provides a robust set of features for web and mobile applications.

name: scikit-learn
description: scikit-learn: machine learning in Python
readmeSummary: scikit-learn is a Python module for machine learning built on top of SciPy and is distributed under the 3-Clause BSD license. It features various classification, regression and clustering algorithms.

name: grpc-go
description: The Go language implementation of gRPC
readmeSummary: The Go language implementation of gRPC. HTTP/2 based RPC. gRPC is a modern open source high performance Remote Procedure Call framework that can run in any environment.

name: angular
description: The modern web developer's platform
readmeSummary: Angular is a platform for building mobile and desktop web applications. Join the community of millions of developers who build compelling user interfaces with Angular.

name: postgres
description: Mirror of the PostgreSQL source code
readmeSummary: PostgreSQL is a powerful, open source object-relational database system with over 35 years of active development that has earned it a strong reputation for reliability, feature robustness, and performance.

name: redwood
description: The App Framework for Startups
readmeSummary: Redwood is an opinionated, full-stack, serverless-ready web framework that allows you to build and deploy with ease. By combining React, GraphQL, Prisma, and TypeScript into one cohesive platform.

name: ethereum
description: Go implementation of the Ethereum protocol
readmeSummary: Go Ethereum is one of the three original implementations of the Ethereum protocol. It is written in Go, fully open source and licensed under the GNU LGPL v3.

name: vite
description: Next generation frontend tooling
readmeSummary: Vite is a build tool that aims to provide a faster and leaner development experience for modern web projects. It consists of a dev server with rich feature enhancements and a build command.

name: numpy
description: The fundamental package for scientific computing with Python
readmeSummary: NumPy is the fundamental package needed for scientific computing with Python. It contains among other things: a powerful N-dimensional array object, sophisticated broadcasting functions.

name: deno
description: A modern runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript
readmeSummary: Deno is a simple, modern and secure runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly that uses V8 and is built in Rust. It provides web platform functionality and APIs.

name: tailwindcss
description: A utility-first CSS framework
readmeSummary: A utility-first CSS framework for rapid UI development. Tailwind CSS is a highly customizable, low-level CSS framework that gives you all of the building blocks you need.

name: quarkus
description: Quarkus: Supersonic Subatomic Java
readmeSummary: Quarkus is a Kubernetes-native Java framework tailored for GraalVM and HotSpot, crafted from best-of-breed Java libraries and standards. Fast boot time, low memory footprint.

issues.title: Dev mode: hot reload not detecting

name: remix
description: Build Better Websites. Create modern, resilient user experiences with web fundamentals.
readmeSummary: Remix is a full stack web framework that lets you focus on the user interface and work back through web standards to deliver a fast, slick, and resilient user experience.

name: poetry
description: Python dependency management and packaging made easy
readmeSummary: Poetry is a tool for dependency management and packaging in Python. It allows you to declare the libraries your project depends on and it will manage them for you.

name: recharts
description: Redefined chart library built with React and D3
readmeSummary: Recharts is a Redefined chart library built with React and D3. The main purpose of this library is to help you to write charts in React applications without any pain.

name: electron
description: Build cross-platform desktop apps with JavaScript, HTML, and CSS
readmeSummary: The Electron framework lets you write cross-platform desktop applications using JavaScript, HTML and CSS. It is based on Node.js and Chromium and is used by the Atom editor and many other apps.

name: aws-sdk-js
description: AWS SDK for JavaScript in the browser and Node.js
readmeSummary: The AWS SDK for JavaScript v3 is a rewrite of v2 with some great new features, including modular architecture and first-class TypeScript support.

name: zig
description: General-purpose programming language and toolchain for maintaining robust, optimal and reusable software
readmeSummary: Zig is a general-purpose programming language and toolchain for maintaining robust, optimal, and reusable software. It aims to replace C as the foundational language of our ecosystem.

name: solid
description: A declarative, efficient and flexible JavaScript library for building user interfaces
readmeSummary: Solid is a declarative JavaScript library for creating user interfaces. It does not use a Virtual DOM. Instead it opts to compile its templates down to real DOM nodes.

name: keras
description: Deep Learning for humans
readmeSummary: Keras is a deep learning API written in Python, running on top of the machine learning platform TensorFlow. It was developed with a focus on enabling fast experimentation.

name: hugo
description: The world's fastest framework for building websites
readmeSummary: Hugo is a static HTML and CSS website generator written in Go. It is optimized for speed, ease of use, and configurability. Hugo takes a directory with content and templates and renders them.

name: postgres-kit
description: The PostgresKit for Node.js
readmeSummary: The fastest full featured PostgreSQL client for Node.js. A performant PostgreSQL driver for Node.js, designed with simplicity, performance and ease of use in mind.

name: bevy
description: A refreshingly simple data-driven game engine built in Rust
readmeSummary: Bevy is a refreshingly simple data-driven game engine built in Rust. It is free and open source. Build game logic with simple Rust functions, then run them in parallel.

name: laravel
description: The PHP Framework for Web Artisans
readmeSummary: Laravel is a web application framework with expressive, elegant syntax. We believe development must be an enjoyable and creative experience to be truly fulfilling.

name: graphql-engine
description: Blazing fast, instant realtime GraphQL APIs on Postgres
readmeSummary: Hasura GraphQL Engine is an ultra-fast, lightweight, web service that gives you instant, realtime, secure GraphQL APIs on all your data sources.

name: mobx
description: Simple, scalable state management
readmeSummary: MobX is a battle tested library that makes state management simple and scalable by transparently applying functional reactive programming. The philosophy behind MobX is very simple.

name: tauri
description: Build smaller, faster, and more secure desktop applications with a web frontend
readmeSummary: Tauri is a framework for building tiny, blazingly fast binaries for all major desktop platforms. Developers can integrate any front-end framework that compiles to HTML, JS and CSS.

name: eslint
description: Find and fix problems in your JavaScript code
readmeSummary: ESLint is a tool for identifying and reporting on patterns found in ECMAScript/JavaScript code, with the goal of making code more consistent and avoiding bugs.

name: rxjs
description: Reactive Extensions Library for JavaScript
readmeSummary: Reactive Extensions Library for JavaScript. RxJS is a library for reactive programming using Observables, to make it easier to compose asynchronous or callback-based code.

name: netty
description: Netty project - an event-driven asynchronous network application framework
readmeSummary: Netty is an asynchronous event-driven network application framework for rapid development of maintainable high performance protocol servers and clients.

name: astro
description: The all-in-one web framework designed for speed
readmeSummary: Astro is the all-in-one web framework designed for speed. Pull your content from anywhere and deploy everywhere, and power even the most dynamic UI with any framework.

name: caddy
description: Fast and extensible multi-platform HTTP/1-2-3 web server with automatic HTTPS
readmeSummary: Caddy 2 is a powerful, enterprise-ready, open source web server with automatic HTTPS written in Go. Extensible with plugins, Caddy simplifies your infrastructure.

name: dayjs
description: 2KB immutable date time library alternative to Moment.js
readmeSummary: Day.js is a minimalist JavaScript library that parses, validates, manipulates, and displays dates and times for modern browsers with a largely Moment.js-compatible API.

name: selenium
description: A browser automation framework and ecosystem
readmeSummary: Selenium is an umbrella project encapsulating a variety of tools and libraries enabling web browser automation. Selenium specifically provides infrastructure for the W3C WebDriver specification.

name: playwright
description: Playwright is a framework for Web Testing and Automation
readmeSummary: Playwright is a framework for Web Testing and Automation. It allows testing Chromium, Firefox and WebKit with a single API. Playwright is built to enable cross-browser web automation.

name: zustand
description: A small, fast and scalable bearbones state-management solution using simplified flux principles
readmeSummary: A small, fast and scalable bearbones state-management solution. Has a comfy API based on hooks, isn't boilerplatey or opinionated, but still opinionated enough to be opinionated.

name: lumen
description: The Laravel Lumen Framework.
readmeSummary: Laravel Lumen is a stunningly fast PHP micro-framework for building web applications with expressive, elegant syntax. We believe development must be an enjoyable experience.

name: material-ui
description: MUI Core: Ready-to-use foundational React components
readmeSummary: MUI offers a comprehensive suite of UI tools to help you ship new features faster. Start with Material UI, our fully-loaded component library, or bring your own design system.

name: celery
description: Distributed Task Queue (development branch)
readmeSummary: Celery is a simple, flexible, and reliable distributed system to process vast amounts of messages, while providing operations with the tools required to maintain such a system.

name: axios
description: Promise based HTTP client for the browser and node.js
readmeSummary: Promise based HTTP client for the browser and node.js. Features include intercepting requests and responses, transforming request and response data, automatic transforms for JSON data.

name: nvm
description: Node Version Manager
readmeSummary: nvm is a version manager for node.js, designed to be installed per-user, and invoked per-shell. nvm works on any POSIX-compliant shell.

name: redux
description: Predictable state container for JavaScript apps
readmeSummary: Redux is a predictable state container for JavaScript apps. It helps you write applications that behave consistently, run in different environments, and are easy to test.

name: postgres-xc
description: PostgreSQL cluster manager
readmeSummary: Postgres-XC is a write-scalable, synchronous, symmetric, and transparent PostgreSQL-based clustering solution that provides both PostgreSQL single-master as well as multi-master write-scalability.

name: prometheus
description: The Prometheus monitoring system and time series database
readmeSummary: Prometheus is an open-source systems monitoring and alerting toolkit originally built at SoundCloud. Since its inception in 2012, many companies and organizations have adopted Prometheus.

name: jquery
description: jQuery JavaScript Library
readmeSummary: jQuery is a fast, small, and feature-rich JavaScript library. It makes things like HTML document traversal and manipulation, event handling, animation, and Ajax much simpler.

name: rust-analyzer
description: A Rust compiler front-end for IDEs
readmeSummary: rust-analyzer is a modular compiler frontend for the Rust language. It is a part of a wider rls-2.0 effort to create excellent IDE support for Rust.

name: styled-components
description: Visual primitives for the component age
readmeSummary: Utilising tagged template literals and the power of CSS, styled-components allows you to write actual CSS code to style your components.

name: symfony
description: The Symfony PHP framework
readmeSummary: Symfony is a PHP framework for web applications and a set of reusable PHP components. Symfony is used by thousands of projects and the community.

name: swagger-ui
description: Swagger UI is a collection of HTML, JavaScript, and CSS assets
readmeSummary: Swagger UI allows anyone to visualize and interact with the API resources without having any of the implementation logic in place.

name: pgbouncer
description: Lightweight connection pooler for PostgreSQL
readmeSummary: PgBouncer is a lightweight connection pooler for PostgreSQL. It is designed to lower the performance impact of opening new connections to PostgreSQL.

name: redis
description: Redis is an in-memory database that persists on disk
readmeSummary: Redis is an in-memory database that persists on disk. The data model is key-value, but many different kind of values are supported: Strings, Lists, Sets, Sorted Sets, Hashes, Streams.

name: docker
description: The Docker CLI and Engine contain the packages for Docker's tooling
readmeSummary: The Docker CLI and Engine contain the packages for Docker's tooling, with which you can develop and containerize your applications and workflows.

name: lodash
description: A modern JavaScript utility library delivering modularity, performance, and extras
readmeSummary: Lodash is a toolkit of JavaScript utility functions that make the basic tasks of working with arrays, numbers, objects, strings, and more easier and cleaner.

name: cargo
description: The Rust package manager
readmeSummary: Cargo downloads your Rust project dependencies and compiles your project. This is the source code for Cargo, the Rust package manager that ships with every Rust installation.

name: webpack-cli
description: CLI for webpack & friends
readmeSummary: webpack CLI provides a flexible set of commands for developers to increase speed when setting up a custom webpack project.

name: yarn
description: The 1.x line is frozen - features and bugfixes now happen on https://github.com/yarnpkg/berry
readmeSummary: Yarn is a package manager for your code. It allows you to use and share code with other developers from around the world.

name: prettier
description: Prettier is an opinionated code formatter
readmeSummary: Prettier is an opinionated code formatter. It enforces a consistent style by parsing your code and re-printing it with its own rules.

name: pip
description: The Python package installer
readmeSummary: pip is the package installer for Python. You can use pip to install packages from the Python Package Index and other indexes.

name: graphql-js
description: A reference implementation of GraphQL for JavaScript
readmeSummary: GraphQL.js is the JavaScript reference implementation for GraphQL, a query language for APIs created by Facebook.

name: socket.io
description: Realtime application framework (Node.JS server)
readmeSummary: Socket.IO enables real-time bidirectional event-based communication. It works on every platform, browser or device, focusing equally on reliability and speed.

name: date-fns
description: Modern JavaScript date utility library
readmeSummary: date-fns provides the most comprehensive, yet simple and consistent toolset for manipulating JavaScript dates in a browser and Node.js.

name: junit5
description: JUnit 5
readmeSummary: JUnit 5 is the next generation of JUnit. The goal is to create an up-to-date foundation for developer-side testing on the JVM.

name: babel
description: Babel is a compiler for writing next generation JavaScript
readmeSummary: Babel is a JavaScript compiler that converts ECMAScript 2015+ code into a backwards compatible version of JavaScript in current and older browsers or environments.

name: numpy-financial
description: Simple financial functions
readmeSummary: Simple financial functions for Python. This is the new home of the deprecated numpy.financial module.

name: rxswift
description: Reactive Programming in Swift
readmeSummary: RxSwift is a Swift version of Rx. It tries to port as many concepts from the original Rx as possible, while introducing improvements in the areas that make sense in Swift.

name: golangci-lint
description: Fast linters Runner for Go
readmeSummary: golangci-lint is a fast Go linters runner. It runs linters in parallel, uses caching, supports yaml config, has integrations with all major IDE and has dozens of linters included.

name: moment
description: Parse, validate, manipulate, and display dates in javascript
readmeSummary: A lightweight JavaScript date library for parsing, validating, manipulating, and formatting dates. The project is in maintenance mode.

name: serde
description: Serialization framework for Rust
readmeSummary: Serde is a framework for ser ializing and de serializing Rust data structures efficiently and generically.

name: mongoose
description: MongoDB object modeling designed to work in an asynchronous environment
readmeSummary: Mongoose is a MongoDB object modeling tool designed to work in an asynchronous environment. Mongoose supports both promises and callbacks.

name: sequelize
description: An easy-to-use and promise-based multi SQL dialect ORM tool for Node.js
readmeSummary: Sequelize is a promise-based Node.js ORM for Postgres, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite and Microsoft SQL Server. It features solid transaction support, relations and more.

name: lottie-web
description: Render After Effects animations natively on Web, Android and iOS, and React Native
readmeSummary: Lottie is a mobile library for Web, and iOS that parses Adobe After Effects animations exported as json with Bodymovin and renders them natively on mobile.

name: r2dbc-postgresql
description: Postgresql R2DBC Driver
readmeSummary: Reactive Relational Database Connectivity Driver Implementation for PostgresSQL using the Project Reactor implementation of R2DBC SPI.

name: immer
description: Create the next immutable state by mutating the current one
readmeSummary: Immer is a tiny package that allows you to work with immutable state in a more convenient way. It is based on the copy-on-write mechanism.

name: testify
description: Go code (golang) set of packages that provide many tools for testifying that your code will behave as you intend
readmeSummary: Go code that provides a set of comprehensive testing tools for use with the normal Go testing system. A collection of assertion helpers and mocks.

name: commander
description: The complete solution for node.js command-line interfaces
readmeSummary: The complete solution for Node.js command-line interfaces, inspired by Ruby's commander. Features include git-style subcommands, option parsing and help generation.
